quote: I woke up and I had been having a dream ... and thought was: “Oh my gosh - I was just dreaming about you” but it wasn't about him exactly - his mom and bro and their kitchen.
gist: She wakes up nose-to-nose with the guy she'd literally just been dreaming about (really about his mom and brother) and gushes that she was just dreaming about him.
visual: A woman waking tangled up with a sleepy man, beaming right into his face — “I was just dreaming about you!” — like the mortifying bedroom version of passing someone on the street who says ‘I was just thinking about you.’
why: HER FLAGGED EXAMPLE #1 (and #3): earnest-awkward irony, and she flags it herself — same entry says ‘I thought of a joke that I thought was really funny.’

quote: About the head thing: able to be hypocritical in his sleep. That's a real power.
gist: A guy who'd insisted that very day he's always careful never to hit his head then bonks his head against her while cuddling in his sleep — she marvels that he managed to be a hypocrite while unconscious.
visual: A couple asleep; the man — who bragged he never hits his head — has just knocked his skull into hers with a little impact-star, her eyes popping open, unimpressed.
why: HER FLAGGED EXAMPLE #2: pure dramatic irony — 'hypocritical in his sleep.' (Setup is referenced, not verbatim; this is her punchline for it.)

quote: plants stealing coins in the middle of the night ... then they go back to their pots, and pretend like nothing happened ‘omg - they are so mean’ ... see the sickening smile on their little face.
gist: She becomes convinced the potted garden plants sneak out through the kitchen window at night to steal coins, then climb back into their pots and act innocent.
visual: Potted houseplants tiptoeing back in through a kitchen window at midnight clutching gold coins, wearing tiny guilty smiles, settling primly back into their pots.
why: Absurd anthropomorphic conspiracy — she narrated and drew the whole heist herself.

quote: I want to go to her house and see what she keeps in the vegetable drawer. Then I could memorize it. Then I could know what to eat.
gist: To justify a crush-stalking, she fantasizes about getting into Frankie's fridge to memorize the contents of her vegetable drawer so she can start eating the exact same things.
visual: Sage crouched with a flashlight, head deep in an open fridge, studiously memorizing the vegetables in someone else's crisper drawer.
why: Deadpan stalker logic — she flags it herself: 'hahahaha... 100% true... not in a weird way.'

quote: I showed him I was calling 911 on my giant walkie talkie phone ... I had to tell the 911 operator, if she could possibly bring, like, one police officer ... not like a whole SWAT team.
gist: In a dream an intruder won't leave, so she calls 911 on a giant walkie-talkie phone and politely requests just one cop, not a whole SWAT team — and then the police handcuff the wrong man.
visual: A woman holding an oversized walkie-talkie phone to her ear, an intruder looming behind her, one finger raised politely: 'just the one officer, please.'
why: Absurd politeness in a crisis; dream logic played completely straight.

quote: bags piled so high that I couldn't see, and a watermelon came crashing down on my head ... pattern: I get hurt in the backseat.
gist: Packing the car for a backpacking trip, the load is stacked so high she can't see, and a watermelon tumbles off the pile and clonks her on the head in the backseat.
visual: A girl buried in the backseat under a teetering tower of camping gear as a watermelon drops straight onto her head.
why: Pure slapstick, plus her wry running gag: 'I always get hurt in the backseat.'

quote: me praying next to a bed ‘thank you god’ for giving me two boyfriends ... eyes closed Christian, eyes open witchcraft.
gist: She pitches a reel of herself kneeling by the bed thanking God for two boyfriends, cutting between a wholesome daytime Christian version and a candlelit witchcraft version.
visual: Split panel of the same girl kneeling at her bedside — bright, pious, eyes closed on one side; dark, crystals and candles, eyes open on the other.
why: Self-aware sacred/profane whiplash — she calls it 'kinda funny...?' herself.

quote: I caught myself being like ‘Oh, I don't want to be here’ ... and then I heard myself saying over the loudspeaker in my head: ‘Yes! I love these kinds of places - ooh! Twine!’
gist: Hating being in the Dollar Tree, she overrides her own bad mood with a chipper PA-announcer voice in her head hyping up the merchandise.
visual: A woman standing dead-eyed in a dollar-store aisle while a loudspeaker mounted in her head booms 'OOH! TWINE!'
why: The absurd gap between real feeling and a forced internal cheerleader.

quote: She was mad at her roommates for coming in her room and drew a picture of her jumping on them - haha! - it was amazing.
gist: Reading her mom's old El Salvador journals, she finds a drawing her mom made of herself leaping onto the roommates who'd invaded her room.
visual: A childlike journal sketch of a furious woman caught mid-pounce, landing on a pile of startled roommates.
why: Petty revenge fantasy immortalized as a gleeful little drawing — she's delighted by it.

quote: in my panic, I threw a pink plate of random half-discarded leftovers at it, and the squirrel laid on the plate to devour it, but it's face was too long.
gist: A squirrel invades her room; she panics and flings a plate of leftovers at it, and the squirrel just flops belly-down onto the plate to eat, its face grotesquely too long.
visual: A long-faced squirrel sprawled flat on a pink plate of leftovers, contentedly munching, in the middle of a bedroom floor.
why: Dream-absurd image anchored by one wrong detail — the too-long face.

quote: there was a toaster-looking heater who was programmed with AI ... talking about thinking it may have been programmed wrong, because it shouldn't get this hot, and I was mildly impressed it had the bandwidth to self-diagnose.
gist: She dreams of a friendly AI toaster-heater that overheats and frets aloud that it might have been programmed wrong — and she's genuinely impressed it can self-diagnose.
visual: A glowing toaster-shaped space heater with a worried little face murmuring 'I really shouldn't be getting this hot...'
why: Deadpan sympathy for a neurotic appliance.

quote: Also dreamt that J went to walk his golden dog - made out of solid gold.
gist: She dreams her sort-of-boyfriend takes his dog out for a walk — a dog made of solid gold.
visual: A man strolling down the sidewalk walking a rigid, gleaming solid-gold dog on a leash.
why: A one-line absurd image delivered totally straight.

quote: reminisced about when bob saget came to my house and was as tall as my stairs.
gist: She fondly recalls Bob Saget visiting her house and being exactly as tall as her staircase.
visual: Bob Saget standing in a foyer, comically as tall as the entire flight of stairs beside him.
why: Surreal scale detail dropped as a warm childhood memory.

quote: the bathrooms were closed ... so we sat by the not working drinking fountain and Wes asked if I could hold his hand while he peed (so he'd feel comfortable, and look into his eyes lol.
gist: With the beach bathrooms closed, her friend Wesley asks her to hold his hand and gaze into his eyes while he pees so he'll feel comfortable.
visual: Two friends by a broken drinking fountain holding hands and locking eyes — while he discreetly pees.
why: Absurd, over-earnest intimacy in the least romantic possible setting; she tags it 'lol.'
